signals.reasoning: Cover the live SOL short immediately. The position is now about $72.79 underwater after previously being up about $37.42, retained none of peak P&L, is flagged urgent, still has no stop, and liquidation distance is under 1%, so this is a failed short that should be cleaned up rather than defended.

BTC still shows a trend-supported rebound with price and open interest both higher on the 24h lookback. Under CAUTION there is no reason to force a fresh long here, but it is also not a clean short while the broader squeeze structure remains intact.

ETH still looks structurally firm after the rebound, with price and open interest both higher and no clean bearish failure yet. It stays watch-only rather than actionable in a position-management round.

TST remains a strong squeeze continuation name, but it is already very extended with huge open interest expansion and positive funding. That makes it too crowded to chase and too unstable to fade fresh.
FARTCOIN is still trending higher with price and open interest both up, but the move is already well underway and crowding is elevated. It is not cleaner than simply fixing the live SOL risk first.
LDO has momentum, but the move is already stretched and the open-interest history here is too incomplete to trust for a fresh call. Under CAUTION this is watch-only.
GRIFFAIN looks more like a completed long-liquidation flush than a clean fresh short. After the drop and with limited quality on the board, I would rather pass than chase.

RESOLV has some constructive signs with price and open interest both slightly up and predicted funding softening, but the edge is not strong enough to matter during active position management. It stays on the watchlist rather than becoming a new trade.
ZEREBRO has upside momentum, but it is already extended and the open-interest support is too incomplete to make the setup trustworthy. Not worth forcing under CAUTION.
KNEIRO is another already-extended squeeze name with incomplete open-interest support. It is interesting for monitoring, but not a cleaner action than flattening SOL.
XPL has rebound momentum, but the chart is already stretched and the supporting open-interest history is too thin to trust. It stays sidelined.
